purpose: Manage an active job search as a kanban pipeline with stats, a detail panel for the selected role, and an applications-per-week chart.
primaryUserGoal: Track roles from Applied through Interviewing to Offer or Rejected without dropping any.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to track job applications, build a pipeline of roles, or review open positions they're considering.
supports: Stats (total applied, interviewing, offers, rejection rate)
Kanban with columns Applied / Interviewing / Offer / Rejected
Detail panel with company info, salary, role, requirements
idealDataShape: Per-job: company, role, salary range, location, stage

populationInstructions.summary: Lead with stats. Include a bar-chart of applications by week. The split section has a kanban on the left (4 columns) and a detail-panel for the selected job on the right with company/salary/role/requirements.
steps: Compute stats: total applied, currently interviewing, offers, rejection rate.
Build a bar-chart with 4-6 weeks of application counts.
Populate the kanban with columns: Applied, Interviewing, Offer, Rejected (tone-coded).
Each card: title=role, subtitle=company, chips for salary + location, footer for stage meta (e.g., 'round 2').
Detail panel for the highest-interest active role: fields for company, salary, role summary, requirements.
Include chips for remote/hybrid, seniority, and fit score.
guardrails: Don't auto-move a job to 'Rejected' without explicit input.
Keep card titles short — full job descriptions belong in the detail panel.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_board_cards to add new applications
Use set_detail when the user picks a different job
replaceTriggers: User pivots to a different career track (ICs to managers, etc.)
